Added field-kit shell, hero, script cards, checklist buttons, score card, and rubric table styling using the app's shadcn-style primitives.
Verified format, typecheck, lint, production build with 46 pages, dev restart, and browser DOM smoke for /field-kit.
Files: npm run format:check; npm run typecheck; npm run lint; npm run build; http://localhost:3000/field-kit

Details: Corrected the field-kit progress card copy so it describes the 72-hour execution checklist instead of implying Day 10 rubric signals were already validated.
Reworked the full product entry preview on / to use the local shadcn-style Badge, Button, Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, and CardDescription primitives instead of the older raw panel/button structure.
Installed the official shadcn/ui dependency stack and converted the local primitives to shadcn-compatible generated components using Radix Slot, CVA variants, clsx, and tailwind-merge.
Files: apps/web/package.json; package-lock.json; components.json; apps/web/lib/utils.ts; apps/web/components/ui/button.tsx; apps/web/components/ui/badge.tsx; apps/web/components/ui/card.tsx

Details: Pinned react and react-dom to the same version after npm dependency resolution created a React version mismatch during production build.
Redesigned the / product preview into a visibly richer shadcn-powered command center with route cards, icon surfaces, a live-drop card, improved navigation, and a more premium onboarding rail.
Ran the official shadcn CLI inside apps/web and generated button, card, badge, input, label, separator, and tabs components with Tailwind v4/PostCSS support.
Files: apps/web/components.json; apps/web/components/ui/button.tsx; apps/web/components/ui/card.tsx; apps/web/components/ui/badge.tsx; apps/web/components/ui/input.tsx; apps/web/components/ui/label.tsx; apps/web/components/ui/separator.tsx; apps/web/components/ui/tabs.tsx; apps/web/postcss.config.mjs; apps/web/app/globals.css

Details: Rebuilt the / product preview with official shadcn components, Tailwind utility classes, route cards, tabs, inputs, labels, separators, badges, and a three-column app dashboard layout.
Fixed the shadcn homepage overflow by preventing legacy global h1/h2/p CSS from overriding Tailwind classes and by delaying the three-column dashboard layout until xl screens.

Redesigned / from an internal command-center into a clearer product home with top navigation, primary save action, four-step user flow, live drop card, persona tabs, core surface cards, and a separate quick setup/onboarding panel.
Simplified / into a cleaner user-attraction flow with a short hero, one primary save action, three-step value path, four core surface cards, and a compact demo setup card.
Added a sticky global navbar in the root layout so users can instantly return Home or jump to Save, Map, Live, Book, and Rewards from any page.
Changed the app theme tokens to the default neutral shadcn palette and reduced custom Rasa color accents on the home page for a stricter shadcn look.

Applied mobile fixes for the checklist pages: global overflow guard, mobile navbar stacking, analytics row cards, restaurant dashboard table cards, field-kit wrapping, and future phase page small-screen tightening.
Added dining imagery to the home page hero, simple flow cards, and product surface cards while keeping the shadcn layout clean and mobile responsive.
Changed the global navbar to a two-row mobile layout with a compact grid of route links so users can always see Home, Save, Map, Live, Book, and Rewards without horizontal clipping.

Added a reusable VisualImage/HeroMedia layer and responsive imagery across Save, Map, Feed, Places, Creators, Booking, Live, Rewards, restaurant tools, reviews, trust, group planning, field kit, and future phase shells.
Audited 43 browser routes for runtime errors and image presence, then added responsive HeroMedia visuals to Phase 0 QA and Phase 1 QA so every route has visual treatment.
Changed Universal Save so users can paste only an Instagram or creator link and save it immediately as a pending recommendation when the restaurant cannot be identified yet.
Clarified the plain Reel link flow by showing pending Instagram saves on the map as an auto-resolving queue that explains the metadata/OCR/AI process before a restaurant becomes a map pin.

Item: Saturday reminder and personal map list
Details: Added a Saturday saved-spots reminder surface and changed the map into a full personal list that includes both mapped spots and auto-resolving plain Reel links.

Added a resolver pipeline for plain Instagram Reel links: shared shortcode extraction, demo fixture matching, optional Meta oEmbed API route, save-flow resolver call, and automatic saved-record update when a place resolves.

Added Next runtime globals to ESLint, verified the resolver API, tested a resolvable plain Instagram Reel through Save into Map, and confirmed unknown Reel links stay in the auto-resolving personal list.
